title: Tender Smoky Greek Pork Gyro
description: A delicious and smoky Greek dish featuring tender pork, wrapped in a warm pita with crunchy vegetables and creamy tzatziki sauce, perfect for a flexitarian meal. This dish is made with strong white flour and namul-blanch-seasoned ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture all over the pork shoulder, making sure to coat it evenly.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, then transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake the pork for 2 hours, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 190 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the pork is cooking, prepare the tzatziki sauce according to the package instructions.
Warm the pita bread by wrapping it in foil and heating it in the oven for 5 minutes.
Slice the cooked pork into thin strips and assemble the gyro by spreading tzatziki sauce on the pita, then adding the pork, tomato, red onion, and cucumber.
